retrieval_text: Comfrey is a medicinal herb profile in the Herbalisti historical corpus. It is represented across 116 works and 203 source-linked chunks. Recorded variants include Comfrey. Common plant-part co-mentions include Root, Leaf, Flower, Bark, Seed, Tops. Common preparation co-mentions include Syrup, Powder, Tea, Decoction, Oil, Infusion. Common condition co-mentions include Cough, Cold or catarrh, Wounds or ulcers, Diarrhoea or looseness, Consumption, Inflammation. Caution co-mentions include Dose caution, Bleeding caution, Toxicity watch, Narcotic or sedative caution, Child-use caution, Allergy or sensitivity caution. Representative source works include The pharmacopoeia universalis or, complete encyclopedia of the materia medica; contained in the pharmacopoeias of London, Edinburgh, and Dublin as well as of all those of Europe and America ; and of the dispensatories, formularies and chemical works ... With an appendix ... / by A. J. L. Jourdan : Edited by J. Rennie.; The occult family physician and botanic guide to health : comprising a description of many American and foreign plants, and their medical virtues, with the cause, cure, and prevention of disease : to which is added, an explanation of the hidden forces in nature, with a large number of valuable receipts, the experience of twenty years' practice; Carleton's household physician : a family medical adviser, containing knowledge that will promote health, cure disease and prolong life, describing all diseases and teaching how to cure them by the simplest medicines : also, an analysis of everything related to courtship, marriage, and the production, management, and rearing of healthy families, together with a chapter on the preparation of medicines, giving prescriptions and valuable receipts, with a section explaining the botanical practice and the popular use of herbs; The American family physician, or, Domestic guide to health : prepared expressly for the use of families, in language adapted to the understanding of the people : arranged in two divisions. This is a retrieval summary of historical co-mentions only, not a medical claim.
